compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PLAN VERIFIED CLEAN (evidence for the operator-attested import AC). After importing 17 resources into rebar state, `terraform plan` = **1 to add, 10 to change, 0 to destroy, 0 to replace**:
- add: aws_ssm_parameter.rebar_secrets["/rebar/prod/rebar-bot-signing-key"] — pre-existing intended placeholder (commit 586302cf2), created as CHANGEME.
- change (all in-place, non-functional): retag project=snap-demo/managed-by/root -> Project=rebar on the 9 taggable adopted resources; aws_iam_role.gha_terraform_plan assume-policy re-render (now references the managed OIDC .arn, resolves to the identical ARN+conditions); aws_route53_record.cert_validation gets allow_overwrite=true (TF-only meta, no DNS change); aws_lambda_function.auth_host code redeploy from rebar's copied source (verified byte-behavior-identical to the deployed zip — the checked-in source was prettier-reformatted after the last deploy; trailing-comma/quote-style only, no logic change — REQUIRED so state source_code_hash matches the committed archive and drift CI stays clean).
- NO secret rotation: random_password.auth_origin_secret imported with its live value (ignore_changes=[special] suppresses the import-default replacement); cookie-signing SSM value preserved via ignore_changes=[value]. Verified live ORIGIN_SECRET matches state; cookie value untouched.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
APPLY COMPLETE + HEALTH VERIFIED (operator-attested evidence). `terraform apply` succeeded; final `terraform plan` = **'No changes. Your infrastructure matches the configuration.'** All 17 resources adopted into rebar state (rebar-tfstate-896586841071), retagged Project=rebar. Pre-existing /rebar/prod/rebar-bot-signing-key drift also resolved (it existed in AWS but not state — imported + tagged). Post-migration health check:
- GET https://auth.solutions.navateam.com/authorize?return_to=https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com -> HTTP 302 to accounts.google.com with client_id 114566571260-... , hd=navapbc.com, and a valid signed state (proves imported origin-secret handshake + preserved cookie-signing secret both work).
- Direct execute-api hit (bypassing CloudFront) -> HTTP 403 (origin-secret guard intact).
- https://auth.solutions.navateam.com/_logout -> HTTP 200 over wildcard-cert TLS.
Next: terraform state rm from snap's shared state (single-owner).

compiled_state.description:
## Why
rebar depends on the GitHub OIDC provider that snap's dead terraform owns, and the domain-wide wildcard cert + `auth_host` SSO stack are to be kept alive under rebar (user decision). All must move from snap's `snap-demo-tfstate` shared state into rebar's `rebar-tfstate` state, owned by rebar's `infra/terraform`. Physical names are preserved (rename = destroy/recreate); only ownership + tags move.

## What
**Provider declarations** (`infra/terraform/versions.tf`): add `hashicorp/random` (~> 3.6) and `hashicorp/archive` (~> 2.4) to `required_providers` alongside the existing `hashicorp/aws` — `random_password` and `archive_file` below require them, or `terraform init`/`validate` fail.

**`infra/terraform/oidc.tf`**: `aws_iam_openid_connect_provider.github` (url `https://token.actions.githubusercontent.com`, client_id `sts.amazonaws.com`, thumbprint `6938fd4d98bab03faadb97b34396831e3780aea1`), `tags = { Project = "rebar" }`. Rewire `iam.tf`'s `gha_terraform_plan_assume` federated identifier to `aws_iam_openid_connect_provider.github.arn`.

**`infra/terraform/acm.tf`**: `aws_acm_certificate.wildcard` (`*.solutions.navateam.com`, DNS), `aws_route53_record.cert_validation` (for_each), `aws_acm_certificate_validation.wildcard`. Cert gets `tags = { Project = "rebar" }` and `lifecycle { prevent_destroy = true }` (shared prod-critical). Validation records use rebar's existing `var.dns_zone_id`.

**`infra/terraform/auth_sso.tf`**: SSO `locals` (auth_host, cookie_domain, google/cookie secret names, google_client_id, TTL). Manage `aws_ssm_parameter.cookie_signing_secret` at its EXISTING path `/auth-solutions/COOKIE_SIGNING_SECRET` with `value = "CHANGEME"` + `lifecycle { ignore_changes = [value] }` (rebar's ssm.tf idiom) — the live value is preserved on import and the Lambda reads it at runtime, so terraform never needs the value. DROP snap's `random_password.cookie_signing_secret` and the `data.aws_ssm_parameter.cookie_signing_secret` (only the edge-gate baking needed it; we do not adopt the sso-gate). Keep `data.aws_ssm_parameter.google_client_secret` (metadata only).

**`infra/terraform/auth_host.tf`**: `random_password.auth_origin_secret` (KEPT — its value must be identical in the Lambda `ORIGIN_SECRET` env and the CloudFront custom origin header; imported with its LIVE value so both sides stay matched and nothing rotates), `data.archive_file.auth_host`, `data.aws_kms_alias.ssm`, `aws_iam_role.auth_host` + `aws_iam_role_policy.auth_host` (least-privilege, verbatim from snap: own log group + `ssm:GetParameters` on the two `/auth-solutions/*` params + `kms:Decrypt` via `ssm.<region>`), `aws_lambda_function.auth_host`, `aws_apigatewayv2_api/_integration/_route/_stage`, `aws_lambda_permission.auth_host_apigw`, cache/origin-request policy data sources, `aws_cloudfront_distribution.auth_host` (`lifecycle { prevent_destroy = true }`, viewer cert = the adopted wildcard), `aws_route53_record.auth_host_a` + `_aaaa`. Tag the taggable resources (Lambda, IAM role, API, CloudFront) `Project = "rebar"`.

**Lambda source**: copy the live-deployed auth-host source from the snap checkout `~/snap-oakhart-manual/snap-oakhart-manual/infra/shared/auth/` — specifically `auth-host/index.js` (306 LOC) and `lib/cookie.js` (114 LOC), both verified present — into `infra/terraform/auth/auth-host/index.js` and `infra/terraform/auth/lib/cookie.js` (consumed by `archive_file.auth_host`). These are the exact files the live `snap-demo-auth-host` Lambda runs.

**Name preservation**: `locals { legacy_name_prefix = "snap-demo" }` with a prominent comment that changing it destroys+recreates 18 live resources. Physical names stay `snap-demo-*`.

## Live migration procedure (operator-attested)
Live IDs (captured): OIDC `arn:…:oidc-provider/token.actions.githubusercontent.com`; ACM `…certificate/6597ee19-a770-4524-a0bd-fb86d608dd99`; Lambda `snap-demo-auth-host`; API `q8mv4bhkmh`; CloudFront `E15OTAFJILDOX6`.
1. `terraform import` each live object into rebar state. Imports are idempotent and non-destructive; on partial failure, re-run the remaining imports (or `state rm` a bad one and retry) — nothing is destroyed until `apply`.
2. Import `random_password.auth_origin_secret` with its live value read at import time from the live Lambda `ORIGIN_SECRET` env into a shell variable (never committed / not in HCL).
3. `terraform plan` and iterate the HCL until the ONLY diff is the retag (drop snap default_tags `project/managed-by/root`, add `Project=rebar`) plus the incidental pre-existing `/rebar/prod/rebar-bot-signing-key` create. NO destroy/replace of adopted resources, NO secret-value change. Record the plan as a ticket comment.
4. `terraform apply` (retag only). Then `terraform state rm` the 18 adopted resources from snap's `infra/shared` state, and remove/neutralize their HCL in snap's dead repo so its config cannot recreate them (snap is decommissioned; its drift CI is off).
5. Post-migration health check: confirm `https://auth.solutions.navateam.com/authorize?return_to=https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com` still issues a 302 to Google and the CloudFront distro + Lambda are healthy (the stack must survive the ownership move).

## Scope
Terraform + Lambda source + live import/apply/state-rm only. Docs are child 6725. Does NOT adopt snap's `github_deploy` role, audit-dashboard sso-gate, ECS/ECR/VPC/RDS.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `versions.tf` declares `hashicorp/random` + `hashicorp/archive`; `aws_iam_openid_connect_provider.github` is in `infra/terraform` tagged `Project=rebar` and the drift role references its `.arn`.
- [ ] Wildcard cert + auth_sso + auth_host resources exist in `infra/terraform` with physical names preserved, `Project=rebar` tags where supported, and `prevent_destroy` on the cert + CloudFront.
- [ ] Cookie-signing SSM param is managed at `/auth-solutions/COOKIE_SIGNING_SECRET` with `ignore_changes=[value]`; the only remaining `random_password` is `auth_origin_secret`.
- [ ] `terraform fmt -check`, `terraform validate`, and `make lint` pass on the new files; `infra/terraform/auth/auth-host/index.js` + `auth/lib/cookie.js` exist.
- [ ] [operator-attested] All 18 resources imported into rebar state; `terraform plan` shows no destroy/replace and no secret-value change (evidence: plan output as a comment).
- [ ] [operator-attested] `terraform apply` completed (retag); resources removed from snap's shared state; `https://auth.solutions.navateam.com/authorize` still returns a 302 to Google post-migration (evidence: outputs as a comment).

file_impact.reason: new: cookie-signing SSM secret + SSO locals
rewire drift role to reference OIDC .arn

compiled_state.title: Adopt snap shared resources into rebar terraform + live import/apply/retag
